panels.description: Wide shot: The Time Traveller’s hands delicately adjusting brass levers on the time machine console. Blurred, ethereal streaks of light background indicating the passage of time. Focus on the vintage metallic machinery.

bubbles.text: These, too, changed and passed. Very gently, I slowed the mechanism down.

panels.description: Medium shot: Inside the lab, Mrs. Watchett is seen moving backwards, her feet floating slightly above the ground, surreal atmosphere. She is gliding toward a door with her back leading the way. The lighting is cold and scientific.

bubbles.text: An odd thing... Mrs. Watchett, moving in reverse, gliding back into the past.

panels.description: Wide shot: The Time Traveller sitting in his machine as it comes to a grinding halt. The lab is dusty and still. Hillyer is a ghostly, blurred silhouette just out of reach. The machine emits a soft glow.
Close-up: The Time Traveller’s face, pale and sweaty, leaning against the cold metal of the machine. He looks disoriented and sickly, eyes unfocused. Heavy shadows accentuate his exhaustion.

bubbles.text: My brain... stagnant. Sick. So weak.

panels.description: Medium shot: The Time Traveller standing by the open door of his laboratory, looking out at his guests (The Medical Man, The Editor, The Journalist). He gestures dismissively with one hand, a mysterious, enigmatic expression on his face.

bubbles.text: I cannot expect you to believe it. Take it as a lie—or a prophecy. It was all a dream, an artful fiction.
